essential_question: Now that the Final is behind you — what does looking back tell you?
objective: Debrief the Final Exam's hardest sections, reflect on growth across the year from Lesson 1 to now, and celebrate what got built.

content.prompt: The Final is done. Before anything else — how do you feel, honestly?

content.heading: Fix it now, while it's fresh
Look back across the whole year

content.preview: Next: Weeks 39–40 — the Capstone Build, then the Portfolio Show & Transcript Close.

success_criteria.text: I can identify what specifically made the hardest exam section hard, and correct it
I can articulate concrete growth from Week 1 to the Final Exam

questions.prompt: Which section of the Final Exam felt hardest, and why?
Pick one problem from the hardest section. Redo it now, correctly, and write what you understand about it now that you didn't mid-exam.
Think back to Week 1 — you freehand-drew a point, ray, segment, and line. Compare that to a problem you solved on this Final. What's different about how you think now?
Name one lesson this year where something genuinely 'clicked.' Describe the moment.
Exit: what's one geometry skill you want to keep sharp before your next math course?
